xfail.analyzeString-020: requires schema-validated PSVI type annotation: the built-in fn:analyze-string-result schema types @nr as xs:positiveInteger, but helium leaves the constructed attribute untyped without a compiled schema
xfail.analyzeString-021: requires schema-validated PSVI type annotation: the built-in fn:analyze-string-result schema gives the result element a named complex type, but helium leaves it xs:untyped without a compiled schema
xfail.json-to-xml-016: requires schemaImport/PSVI type annotation: fn:json-to-xml(...,map{'validate':true()}) should validate against the built-in schema so j:number is typed xs:double, but helium leaves it untyped, so data(...) instance of xs:double is false
xfail.fn-namespace-uri-16: helium fn:namespace-uri returns xs:string, not xs:anyURI, so assert-type xs:anyURI fails on the empty-string result
xfail.fn-min-14: helium fn:min returns xs:integer, losing the selected item's derived integer subtype: min((xs:positiveInteger(123),xs:unsignedShort(124))) is not instance of xs:nonNegativeInteger
xfail.fn-min-15: helium fn:min returns xs:integer, losing the selected item's xs:positiveInteger subtype, so assert-type xs:positiveInteger fails
xfail.fn-min-16: helium fn:min does not convert xs:anyURI to xs:string: min((xs:anyURI('http://a.com'),'http://b.com')) stays xs:anyURI, so assert-type xs:string fails
xfail.fn-max-14: helium fn:max returns xs:integer, losing the selected item's derived integer subtype: max((xs:positiveInteger(123),xs:unsignedShort(124))) is not instance of xs:nonNegativeInteger
xfail.fn-max-15: helium fn:max returns xs:integer, losing the selected item's xs:unsignedShort subtype, so assert-type xs:unsignedShort fails
xfail.fn-max-16: helium fn:max does not convert xs:anyURI to xs:string: max((xs:anyURI('http://c.com'),'http://b.com')) stays xs:anyURI, so assert-type xs:string fails
xfail.fn-current-time-4: helium fn:seconds-from-time returns xs:double, not xs:decimal, so assert-type xs:decimal fails
xfail.fn-current-dateTime-24: helium fn:seconds-from-dateTime returns xs:double, not xs:decimal, so assert-type xs:decimal fails
